Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 3, 065 [Muššu’u 2][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[x x] ⸢saŋ⸣-gig an-ta mu-un-keš₂ ki-a sag₃-ga mu-un;::- ⸢da⸣-ab-zig₃

(o 1) [...]: He assigned headache from heaven (Akkadian: Headache has been assigned from heaven), it has been uprooted from the Underworld with a blow (Akkadian: it is uprooted from the Underworld).

[di]-⸢ʾu⸣ ina ANe ra-kis ina KIti₃ in-na-as-saḫ
o 2o 2

[x] ⸢a₂⸣-tuku a₂-bi <<NU>> mu-un-da-ab-til

(o 2) It has brought the strength of a powerful [...] (Akkadian has: of a strong young man) to an end.

[x] eṭ-lu EN e-mu-qa e-mu-qa-a-šu₂ uq-ta-at-ti
o 3o 3

[ki]-⸢sikil⸣ sag₁₀-ga <<NA>> a₂-bi nu-mu-un-ši-gi₄-gi₄

(o 3) It does not let the power of a beautiful young girl return.

⸢ša₂⸣ ar₂-dat da-mi-iq-tu₄ is-sa ul u₂-ta-ar
o 4o 4

[lu₂]-⸢lu₇⸣lu-bi su-na mi-ni-in-ŋar-re-eš

(o 4) Inana (Akkadian: Ištar), without whom nobody exists in luxuriance and pleasure, brought down from the mountain what they have placed (Akkadian: has been placed) in the body of that man.

⸢ša₂?⸣ ina zu-u₂-ri LU₂ šu-a-tu₄ iš-šak-nu
o 5o 5

[d]⸢inana⸣-ke₄ ḫi-li-bi nam-a-ta ugu-na nu-til-la-še₃  ; kur-ta im-ta-e₃-⸢am₃?⸣

[d]⸢iš⸣-tar ša₂ ina nu-uḫ-šu₂ u ul-ṣu ul-la-nu-uš-šu₂  ; ⸢mam⸣-ma-an la i-ba-aš₂-šu-u₂ iš-tu KUR-šu₂ u₂-še-ri-du
o 6o 6

[ŋeš-ge]-en-ge-na lu₂-lu₇lu pa₄-ḫal mu-un-na-teŋ₃-eš

(o 6) They brought it near (Akkadian: It approached) the limbs of the sick (Akkadian: restless) man.

[a]-⸢na⸣ bi-na-a-tu₂ LU₂ mut-tal-li-ku iṭ-ḫe-e-ma
o 77

[x x] ⸢u₂?⸣-a im-ma-<ni>-in-us₂ : LU₂ šu₂-a-ti₃ u₂?-a i-te-mid

(o 7) It imposed pain [...] (Akkadian: on that man).

o 88

[x x zi]-zi a-ba zig₃-ge-eš : man-nu i-na-as-saḫ man-nu u₂-šat-bi

(o 8) [...] (Akkadian: Who) will uproot (it), who will raise (it)?

o 99

[...] ⸢dsuen⸣-na-ke₄ : diš-tar ma-rat d30

(o 9) [...] (Akkadian: Ištar, the daughter) of Suen (Akkadian: Sin)!

o 1010

[den]-⸢kum₃⸣ dumu den-lil₂-la₂-ke₄ : dMIN<(EN.KUM₃)> mar dMIN<(EN.LIL₂.LA₂.KE₄)>

(o 10) Enkum (Akkadian: Enkummu), the son of Enlil (Akkadian: Ellil)!

o 1111

[dasar-lu₂]-ḫi dumu eridugki-ga-ke₄ : dAMAR.UTU mar eri-du₁₀

(o 11) Asarluhi (Akkadian: Marduk), the son of Eridug (Akkadian: Eridu)!

o 12o 12

[x lu₂]-⸢lu₇⸣lu pa₄-ḫal-la-ke₄ [ḫe₂-eb₂]-⸢ta⸣-zig₃-eš

(o 12) May they raise it [...] of the sick (Akkadian: restless) man.

[x x] ⸢LU₂⸣ mut-⸢tal⸣-[li-ki li]-⸢šat⸣-bu-u₂
o 13o 13

r 1'r 1'

(r 1') $ 1 line traces

[...] x
r 2'r 2'

[...] x x x x x [...] u-me-ni-e₃

(r 2') Thread (on a string) [...] ... [...] (Akkadian: Thread (on a string) [...] serpentine, sangilmud-stone (and) iron).

[x x na₄MUŠ?].GIR₂ na₄san-gil-mud na₄AN.BAR šu-ku-uk-ma
r 3'r 3'

[x x 7]⸢na⸣ a-ra₂ 2na u-me-ni-keš₂

(r 3') Tie 7 times 2 [...].

[x x] ⸢se⸣-bet a-di ši-na ku-ṣur-ma
r 4'4'

[...] u-me-ni-šum₂ : ši-pat e-ri-du i-di-ma

(r 4') Recite [...] (Akkadian: the incantation of Eridu).

r 5'5'

[... u]-⸢me⸣-ni-keš₂ : pu-ut-su ru-ku-us-ma

(r 5') Bind [...] (Akkadian: his forehead).

r 6'r 6'

[...] KAL ul-du₃-a e₂-gar₈-ra-ke₄ i₃-ŋeš ŋešeren teš₂-bi u-me-ni-ḫi-ḫi

(r 6') Mix together cedar oil (and) [...] ... distant times of the wall (Akkadian: [...] of a fly, eggs of a fly, labbinu-fly of the wall).

[x zu]-⸢um⸣-bu pe-lu-u₂ zu-um-bu lab?-bi-nu ša₂ i-ga-ri  ; ⸢ša₂⸣-man e-re-ni iš-te-niš bu-lu-ul-ma
r 7'7'

[... u]-⸢me⸣-ni-šeš₂ : pu-ut-su pu-šu-uš-ma

(r 7') Anoint [...] (Akkadian: his forehead).

r 8'8'

[...] ⸢ḫa⸣-ba-zi-zi : di-i šu-ru-up-pu-u₂  ; ša₂ ina zu-mur-šu₂ li-ne₂-siḫ₂

(r 8') May [...] (Akkadian: the headache (and) chill that are in his body) be uprooted.

r 9'r 9'

[...] ḫe₂-en-sikil-la ḫe₂-en-dadag-ga

(r 9') [...] may he be pure, may he be bright.

[...] x-da-a-tu₂ li-lil li-bi-ib
r 10'r 10'

[...] ⸢diŋir⸣-re-e-ne-še₃ ⸢ḫe₂⸣-en-ši-in-gi₄-gi₄

(r 10') May he return [...] of his gods (Akkadian: May he entrust (himself) [...] the good hands of his god).

[x x] ⸢qa⸣-ti-šu₂ dam-qa-a-tu₂ ša₂ DINGIR-šu₂ li-ip-qid
r 11'11'

[x x] ⸢mu⸣-un-dab saŋ si sa₂ mu-un-dab-dab NA? x x ⸢mu?-un?⸣-dab

(r 11') [...]: It seized (him), put right (his) head, it repeatedly seized (him), it seized ....

r 12'12'

[...] saŋ ke-eš mu-un-dab mu-un-dab mu-un-dab-dab mu-un-dab-dab te EN₂

(r 12') [...], take care (of him), it seized (him), it seized (him), it repeatedly seized (him), it repeatedly seized (him). Incantation formula.

r 13'13'

⸢EN₂⸣ mu-un-dab saŋ si sa₂ mu-un-dab saŋ-ki!(DI) SA KAR RA mu-un-dab

(r 13') Incantation: It seized (him), put right (his) head, it seized (him), it seized (his) ... forehead.

r 14'14'

[... a-da]-pa₃ abgal eridugki-ga-ke₄

(r 14') [...]. Adapa, the sage of Eridug, (and) Asarluhi, take care (of him), it seized (him), it seized (him), it repeatedly seized (him), it seized (him). Incantation formula.

r 15'15'

[dasar]-⸢lu₂⸣-ḫi saŋ ke-eš mu-un-dab mu-un-dab mu-un-dab-dab mu-un-dab te EN₂en-na

Top
(colophon)
t.e. 11

[x x saŋ]-gig ⸢gu₂⸣-sa-a ⸢saŋ?-gig?⸣ gu₂-⸢sa⸣-a

(t.e. 1) [...]: It is headache (and) neck muscles, it is headache (and) neck muscles.

t.e. 22

[...] ⸢m⸣BAša₂-a bu₁₂-kur₂ mdINANA—MU—KAM ŠA₃.BAL.BAL mE₂.KUR—za-kir lu₂DIŠ

(t.e. 2) [...] Iqiša, son of Ištar-šum-ereš, descendant of Ekur-zakir, mašmaššu-priest.
